item.title: Interactively Addressable Organic Metadevices
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-75757-4

An interactively addressable organic metadevice enables independent electrical control of metasurface pixels to generate reconfigurable holographic projections, providing a platform for programmable and user-interactive photonic systems.

item.title: Inhibited Radiative Decay Enhances Single-Photon Emitters
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-75489-5

Spin–photon interfaces for quantum networks often rely on resonator enhanced emission, limiting scalability and requiring precise tuning. Here, the authors demonstrate an alternative approach that avoids these limitations by suppressing unwanted radiative decay channels of erbium dopants using silicon photonic crystal waveguides.

item.title: Chip-Integrated Metasurface-Enabled Single-Photon Skyrmion Sources
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-75516-5

On-chip generation of single-photon skyrmions is challenging due to the nanoscale confinement of quantum emitters. Here the authors demonstrate a metasurface-integrated quantum emitter platform enabling room-temperature on-chip generation of single-photon skyrmions without requiring magnetic fields.

item.title: Two-Dimensional Pixel-Level Addressable Mid-Infrared Metasurface Spatial Light Modulator
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-75346-5

A mid-infrared amplitude metasurface spatial light modulator using phase change materials is demonstrated in a scalable crossbar architecture on a silicon foundry platform, allowing for high density, tunable pixel metasurfaces.

item.title: STARE: A Semiconductor-Integrated Transmit-Array Architecture for 6G Beamforming
item.description:
Nature Communications, Published online: 01 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74911-2

This study presents STARE, a semiconductor integrated reconfigurable intelligent surface that embeds diodes in silicon to steer wireless beams, offering a scalable route for 6G communication systems.
